tiny_practice.action: Close your eyes and visualize Jesus as the Good Shepherd, quietly listening for His guiding voice in your current life situation

two_step.reflect_question: How am I recognizing and responding to Jesus' voice in my daily decisions?

options.text: I often feel uncertain about God's direction
options.response: Start a daily prayer journaling practice to improve spiritual listening skills

options.text: I struggle to distinguish God's voice from other influences
options.response: Commit to reading Scripture daily and comparing inner impressions against biblical principles

options.text: I want to develop deeper spiritual discernment
options.response: Seek mentorship from a mature Christian who can help guide spiritual understanding

creative_prompt.instruction: Draw a sheep following a loving shepherd, showing how closely the sheep stays near its protector

reevaluation.question: How has this chapter changed your understanding of Jesus' relationship with believers?
options: Jesus is more relational than I previously understood
I see Jesus as a protective guide, not just a distant deity
I recognize the importance of listening and following
I feel more confident in my spiritual journey

three_choice.question: Which metaphor of Jesus speaks most powerfully to you?

choices.expansion: Jesus intimately knows and protects His followers, guiding them with personal care.
Jesus represents the exclusive pathway to salvation, defining authentic spiritual access.
Christ's voluntary sacrifice demonstrates ultimate love through intentional self-surrender.

socratic_nudge.question: If a shepherd truly knows each sheep, how deeply does God actually know you, beyond surface-level understanding?

moral_slider.question: How do you navigate the tension between divine protection and human free will?

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of spiritual leadership transformed?
options: I see leadership as sacrificial service
I recognize spiritual authority comes through relationship, not position
I understand vulnerability is a form of strength
I appreciate the difference between hired hands and true shepherds

thematic_summaries.title: The Good Shepherd
thematic_summaries.summary: Jesus describes Himself as the true shepherd who knows and protects His flock. He distinguishes authentic spiritual leadership from false shepherds who exploit and abandon their followers.

thematic_summaries.title: Divine Identity and Salvation
thematic_summaries.summary: Jesus reveals His divine nature and mission of providing abundant life through sacrificial love. He emphasizes His unique relationship with God the Father and His role in eternal salvation.

reevaluation.question: How does Jesus' metaphor of the Good Shepherd challenge traditional religious leadership paradigms?
options: Leadership is about sacrificial service, not personal gain
Spiritual authority derives from intimate relationship, not institutional position
True shepherds prioritize individual care over institutional maintenance
Authentic spiritual guidance requires complete self-surrender
